compiled_state.title: Orchestrator: user requests ticket reformatting -> orchestrator asks for confirmation instead of acting
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
### 2. Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** User-Flagged Behavior

#### Expected Behavior

When a ticket creation warning explicitly states the recommended fix and the user asks to apply it, the orchestrator reformats the title and description immediately without asking for confirmation.

#### Actual Behavior

After creating bug ticket 8985-4efc, the orchestrator received two CLI warnings with explicit fix instructions (title format, description headers). The user said 'yes' (implying apply the fix). The orchestrator responded by asking 'Want me to update it to match the recommended format?' instead of immediately applying the fix.

### 3. Action History and Workarounds

* **Steps Taken:**
  * `.claude/scripts/dso ticket create bug ...` emitted two warnings with exact fix commands
  * User said 'yes' in response to the implied question of whether to fix it
* **Known Workarounds:** User must explicitly say 'yes just do it' or similar to unblock the orchestrator

comments.body: Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Escalated: fix requires edits to CLAUDE.md ('Always Do These' section — new rule: yes-as-authorization) and fix-bug/SKILL.md (post-creation validation, line 184). Both are safeguarded files. Needs explicit user authorization to add: 'When user responds yes/sure/go ahead to an implied action suggestion, treat as authorization and execute immediately — do NOT re-ask.' Diagnosis: Failure Mode 3 (confirmation-seeking overrides user's clear implicit intent).
comments.author: Test
